votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the lowercase ASCII check, the Caesar cipher rotation (rot4), and the string mapping logic defined in the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly implements the string mapping and character rotation logic defined in the Coq specification.
The Python code correctly implements the ROT4 encryption logic for lowercase ASCII characters and maps it over the string, matching the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. Both define a_code=97, z_code=122, check if a character is lowercase ASCII (97-122), apply rot4 transformation (shift by 4 within lowercase letters using modulo 26), and map this function over strings. The encrypt_spec checks if res equals the encrypted string in both versions. The only difference is Python returns bool while Coq uses Prop, but for concrete inputs they produce the same judgments.

The Python code correctly implements the same logic as the Coq code: a_code=97, z_code=122, is_lowercase_ascii checks if character code is in [97,122], rot4_ascii rotates lowercase letters by 4 positions with modulo 26 wrap-around, map_string applies a function to each character, and encrypt_spec checks if res equals the encrypted string. All concrete computations will produce the same results.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. Both define a_code=97, z_code=122, check if a character is lowercase ASCII (97-122), apply rot4 cipher (shift by 4 with modulo 26 wrap), and map this transformation over strings. The encrypt_spec checks if res equals the encrypted string in both versions. The only difference is Python returns bool while Coq uses Prop, but for concrete inputs they produce the same judgments.
Implements the same Caesar shift by 4 for lowercase ASCII (a–z), leaves other chars unchanged, maps per-character over the string, and checks equality; behavior matches Coq on all ASCII inputs.
Both implement a Caesar shift by 4 on ASCII lowercase letters (a–z) and leave other characters unchanged; map over strings and equality checks match the Coq spec.
Python matches Coq’s lowercase check and rot4 transformation and maps it over the string; the equality check mirrors the Coq spec on all ASCII inputs.
